title: John, Chapter 20 (Codex Bezae)
source: Codex Bezae (5th c.) via Cambridge IIIF + NTVMR mapping + Tischendorf + KJV
description: John 20 from Codex Bezae (5th c., Cambridge UL). Page-specific folios via NTVMR.
introduction: John 20 from Codex Bezae (Codex Cantabrigiensis, 5th c.), the most important Western text manuscript. A bilingual Greek/Latin codex containing the Gospels and Acts, held at Cambridge University Library.

translation.original_text: <sup>1</sup>Τῇ<S>3588</S> δὲ<S>1161</S> μιᾷ<S>1520</S> τῶν<S>3588</S> σαββάτων<S>4521</S> Μαρίαμ<S>3137</S> ἡ<S>3588</S> Μαγδαληνὴ<S>3094</S> ἔρχεται<S>2064</S> πρωῒ<S>4404</S> σκοτίας<S>4653</S> ἔτι<S>2089</S> οὔσης<S>1510</S> εἰς<S>1519</S> τὸ<S>3588</S> μνημεῖον,<S>3419</S> καὶ<S>2532</S> βλέπει<S>991</S> τὸν<S>3588</S> λίθον<S>3037</S> ἠρμένον<S>142</S> ἐκ<S>1537</S> τοῦ<S>3588</S> μνημείου.<S>3419</S>
translation.text:
<sup>1</sup>The first
day of the week cometh Mary Magdalene early, when it was yet dark, unto the sepulchre, and seeth the stone taken away from the sepulchre.

translation.text:
<sup>1</sup>The first
day of the week cometh Mary Magdalene early, when it was yet dark, unto the sepulchre, and seeth the stone taken away from the sepulchre. <sup>2</sup>Then she runneth, and cometh to Simon Peter, and to the other disciple, whom Jesus loved, and saith unto them, They have taken away the Lord out of the sepulchre, and we know not where they have laid him. <sup>3</sup>Peter therefore went forth, and that other disciple, and came to the sepulchre. <sup>4</sup>So they ran both together: and the other disciple did outrun Peter, and came first to the sepulchre. <sup>5</sup>And he stooping down,
and looking in, saw the linen clothes lying; yet went he not in. <sup>6</sup>Then cometh Simon Peter following him, and went into the sepulchre, and seeth the linen clothes lie, <sup>7</sup>And the napkin, that was about his head, not lying with the linen clothes, but wrapped together in a place by itself. <sup>8</sup>Then went in also that other disciple, which came first to the sepulchre, and he saw, and believed. <sup>9</sup>For as yet they knew not the scripture, that he must rise again from the dead. <sup>10</sup>Then the disciples went away again unto their own home. <sup>11</sup>But Mary stood without at the sepulchre weeping: and as she wept, she stooped down,
and looked into the sepulchre, <sup>12</sup>And seeth two angels in white sitting, the one at the head, and the other at the feet, where the body of Jesus had lain. <sup>13</sup>And they say unto her, Woman, why weepest thou? She saith unto them, Because they have taken away my Lord, and I know not where they have laid him.

verification.scan_manuscript: Codex Bezae (MS Nn.2.41, 5th c., Cambridge UL)
